rationale: A0 shows schtasks.exe creating a scheduled task named 'SoundIentifier' (deliberate typo to evade detection) that invokes wmic to execute a payload stored in an Alternate Data Stream (ADS) path 'C:\Users\Public\Disk101.ini:59e9c935' — a classic ADS-based payload hiding technique. EV-001 shows a second scheduled task 'Disk Diag' created moments later, set to run every 6 minutes via explorer.exe launching 'C:\Users\Public\Weber0.js', a JavaScript file in the world-writable Public directory. Both tasks are created from the same cmd.exe parent (PID 1592) under corp\Administrator at High integrity, indicating a persistence mechanism being established. The combination of ADS execution, a recurring JS-based task, deceptive task names, and payloads staged in C:\Users\Public strongly indicates post-compromise persistence activity.
